topic: Metadata deep dive — every tag that matters
hook: A supervisor searched 3,000 tracks for "90-second hopeful African instrumental."

slides.eyebrow: Education · Metadata deep dive — every tag that matters
slides.headline: A supervisor searched 3,000 tracks for "90-second hopeful African instrumental."
Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: Your track existed. · She never found it. · Here's why metadata is the invisible infrastructure. ↓
list_items: Your track existed.
She never found it.
Here's why metadata is the invisible infrastructure. ↓

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: WHAT NEEDS TO LIVE IN THE FILE (embedded metadata): · Title: exact, consistent across every platform · Artist/Composer: your name as it appears on your PRO registration · ISRC: your 12-character unique recording identifier
list_items: WHAT NEEDS TO LIVE IN THE FILE (embedded metadata):
Title: exact, consistent across every platform
Artist/Composer: your name as it appears on your PRO registration
ISRC: your 12-character unique recording identifier
BPM: to the decimal if possible (147.5 is more useful than "fast")
Key: standard notation — Cm, Gmaj, F#min
Genre: specific — "Afrofusion" is searchable; "African" is not
Mood: industry vocabulary — cinematic, tense, hopeful, driving, sparse, melancholic
Year: year of recording, not release

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: WHAT NEEDS TO LIVE IN YOUR CATALOGUE SYSTEM: · Duration: exact, to the second · Instrumentation: list every prominent instrument · Vocal vs. instrumental flag
list_items: WHAT NEEDS TO LIVE IN YOUR CATALOGUE SYSTEM:
Duration: exact, to the second
Instrumentation: list every prominent instrument
Vocal vs. instrumental flag
One-stop status: confirmed yes/no with documentation
Stems availability: what exists and how fast you can deliver
PRO affiliation

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: WHAT NEEDS TO LIVE IN YOUR PITCH: · Rights summary: one-stop or split, documented and ready to send · Response time commitment: stated and real · Direct licensing contact
list_items: WHAT NEEDS TO LIVE IN YOUR PITCH:
Rights summary: one-stop or split, documented and ready to send
Response time commitment: stated and real
Direct licensing contact

slides.headline:
Metadata doesn't make your music better.
It makes your music findable.
In every sync database and brief-matching tool — metadata is how your track
Without it, you're invisible.
slides.lede: Your music exists. The brief exists. They never meet.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
